title: Level 611
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 611: "Metathesiophobia"}} {{CSS|User:阁楼福克123/Darkness.css}}
{{Class Variable|safety=Varying Safety|security=Stable|hexagon=💡}} {{Notice|body=This is a remastered version. The original version was deleted sometime between 2 and 3 years ago. The rewrite process is down in the credits.}} ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Mansion Living Room.jpg|caption=An image of the living room in In-Between-State of Level 611}} '''Level 611''', is a massive suburban residence estimated to be around 892,411.76 km² in size,However, it remains to be unproven due to the nature of the level. stretching in all directions. '''Level 611''' was formerly safe for the colonists residing there, but this would all change with [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]]. It was after this event that all the entities were discovered when searching and moving through the southern regions of the level. This event is the strangest and most confusing thing. It is only the survivors before [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]] who were aware of what happened. The level takes place within the interior of a suburban home. This consists of living rooms, kitchens, and bedrooms. Bathroom, offices, and storage are present but rarely. The interior of the homes differs, essentially with rooms that can be anywhere from 20 to 100 feet long, 30 to 90 feet wide, and ceilings that can be anywhere from 6 to 12 feet. The majority of the living room space fills the room, with the storage rooms and kitchens filling a sizable portion of it. Bedrooms, bathrooms, and offices are considerably smaller, covering a small portion of it. Wanderers may exit the indoor section by proceeding north or south, but sometimes the exits lead to the same location, or to a different layout, provisions, or even the entire indoor section. This is usually annoying or confusing. Wanderers often get trapped or may go crazy after opening "EXIT" doors that repeatedly lead nowhere. The environment layout almost resembles [[The Frontrooms]], but other wanderers reported that the rooms are based on memories from childhood. Wanderers have reported that the rooms can resemble spaces that they remember before entering [[The Frontrooms]], but this is not confirmed. When wanderers enter '''Level 611''', they are in a '''In-Between-State''' that is not '''Light Mode''' or '''Dark Mode'''. This mixed state can cause light switches, if they are encountered, to function correctly, but turning them can turn the light dim or normal, balanced light. The provisions in this mixed region are generally minimal, and if the lights are turned off, complete darkness reigns, potentially attracting very scary ahhh entities. There are some doors in the level that lead to different places. When opened, the doors can either open to the same room or to new arrangements, and even to different provisions or settings. These doors, which are visible both in the north and south, serve as a gateway to the '''Light Mode''' and the '''Dark Mode'''. ===Incident=== During [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]], over half of the wanderers were killed by entities such as [[Entity 3|Smilers]] and [[Predatory Doors]]. The number of these entities was so bad that [[Level 611]] became desolate, with parts of it now only inhabited by creatures. After three months, without electricity, [[Level 611]] began to regain electricity, but only the northern part of the level fully reverted back to electricity. As a result, the level began to have a return from wanderers, but fewer than prior to [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]]. Many supplies, such as food, have been used up by the entities that now inhabit the level. Entities such as [[Entity 9|Facelings]] have become extinct or disappeared completely from [[Level 611]]. Since [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]], the level has become more unstable. If it becomes overcrowded by wanderers, the area can destabilize, and windows might randomly open, with the risk of the wanderer being sucked out. The lights may flicker constantly, and some wanderers have been reported to experience visual hallucinations, disorientation, and blurred vision. There are rumors that the level might have been maintained by a sentient entity, or that the level itself is sentient, though this is not proven. ===The Connection Theory=== There is a rumor that '''Level 611''' and [[Level 602]] are related to each other. Both levels have light and dark themes, and [[Level 602]] was formerly a sub-level of '''Level 611'''. There is a rumor that they could be related, but the connection has not been confirmed. ==Modes (Zones)== ===In-Between-State=== '''The In-Between State''', or known as '''"In-Between Mode"''' or the '''"In-Between Zone"''', is a balanced space where light levels fluctuate. The lighting can range from dim to normal to bright, looking at the windows appears to be a forest landscape and or they see a pitch white void or a pitch black void but piles of snow, about 17 cm deep,The pitch white void often occurs near '''Light Mode''', while the pitch black void often occurs near '''Dark Mode'''. and light switch works properly. This is often the first area or Modes (Zones) where wanderers arrive. Although the location is safe, there are certain dangers, especially in the southern areas. If the wanderer goes south to about 50 to 60 meters in these areas, the atmosphere starts to shift. The light starts to get darker, and the temperature drops considerably, and in some cases, sightings of entities occur. But if the traveler heads north to the 50 to 60 meter line, the light gets brighter, and the temperature increases. Objects are scarce, but they can be found in hidden areas. Wanderers have to look cautiously to find them. Sleeping is instinctive here, and wanderers can sleep whenever they desire. ===Light Mode=== {{Blurimage|file=C6AD4B07-1639-455F-B341-0055ED709365(REMAKE).jpg|caption=An Image of Light Mode}} '''Light Mode''', or simply referred to as "'''Light Zone'''", is a region where the lights are too intense to endure, temporarily blinding wanderers for staying too long. The zone uses a combination of lighting, including recessed lights, downlights, and single bulbs, that occasionally flash bright bursts of light. Prolonged exposure can lead to severe visual impairment and mild skin burning. To enter '''Light Mode''', one must wander approximately 100 meters/ the light becomes brighter in increments, and continuous flashes of light are the norm. Windows are going pitch white, and the light switches do not function at all, meaning the light cannot be turned off. Although there are no direct physical hazards anywhere in the zone, the intense brightness is prone to distortion of vision, causing everything to appear blurry. Exposure to it on a continuous basis results in insomnia because wanderers are unable to sleep in the constant light. Some wanderers have reported that they were unable to blink or shut their eyes under certain circumstances. The zone is equipped with objects, including [[Object 1|Almond Water]], [[Object 44|Cashew Water]], and others, but they disappear when a wanderer moves 20 meters south. Before the shift, the objects were freely usable in any direction. Items, upon collection, continue to be stored in a wanderer's inventory even when wandering in the opposite direction. However, moving to the north causes bizarre effects on the mind. ===Dark Mode=== {{Blurimage|file=The darkest hours of Level 611(REMAKE).jpg|caption=An Image of Dark Mode}} '''Dark Mode''', or referred to as '''"Dark Zone"''', is a completely black zone with barely any light scattered throughout the zone. The light sources are difficult to find and are only available at specific locations, and therefore it is recommended that one carry a flashlight. As wanderers travel south, around 100 meters walking, the level gradually becomes completely dark. The lights fade and are barely able to stay lit, dropping to zero until they go out completely. Entities gradually begin appearing in the level.The majority of the entities hide in small spaces. When looking through windows, they gradually become completely black, and the lights are not able to turn on by the light switches. There are a few faint light sources hidden around, but they are rare. These sources emit an orange tint. From the outside, the snowy neighborhood is replaced by a pitch-black void, which is commonly linked to [[Entity 2|Windows]] or rarely a void filled with random objects such as cars, houses, bricks, pens, paper, trees, and laptops just floating in place. Entities spawn at an abnormal rate. Most items will dematerialize when facing and walking south, except for food items, which do not decompose. Food, however, will almost immediately grow mold, algae, and various fungi. The temperature of the level is cold, around -2°C (24.8°F), which poses a risk of hypothermia. While facing and walking south, one will feel fear and pain. In rare cases, individuals may develop schizophrenia. Food used to never expire here, which made the level a hotspot for colonization and outposts. However, most entities are highly aggressive, often attacking each other over food or territory. Sleeping in this zone is possible but not recommended, as constant nightmares often occur for the wanderer who sleeps in the zone. These nightmares typically involve events that the person has never experienced. Many wanderers have reported hallucinations of childhood memories that they don't recognize. Often hearing disturbing sounds such as screaming or crying, but there's no clear source of these noises. Some [[M.E.G.]] teams have been dispatched to establish a fully operational base in '''Dark Mode''' but were not able to do so. The M.E.G. reported that if they face north, it causes bizarre effects on their mind, and if they face south, it causes paranoia. Although with these effects, they were still able to build a base for a brief period of only around 7 minutes before being attacked by entities. The M.E.G. determined that an outpost of this level is practically out of the question. ==Entities== Most of the entities are seen when wandering and heading south towards '''Dark Mode'''. Some of the entities in an '''In-Between State''' have been seen on occasion, especially when wandering more south. There have been [[Phenomenon 35|Wretch]] sightings when in '''Light Mode'''. The light seems to burn and vaporize the [[Phenomenon 35|Wretch]], assumed to be heat or radiation, to ash before they can do any damage to anything. These entities can include, but are not limited to: ===Windows=== '''Windows''' are different from predatory windows in other levels. '''Windows''' are usually pitch black, an empty void. If the window is open or broken, it will attempt to pull the wanderer in and send them to [[The Void]]. Windows are most commonly found in the '''Dark Mode''' but can happen occasionally in the '''In-Between State''', especially when moving south. The windows in this zone increasingly become pitch black. ===Predatory Doors=== '''Predatory Doors''' are most commonly seen throughout in '''Dark Mode'''. Predatory Doors are most commonly labeled as "EXIT" doors, which are usually indoors and left and right. They lure wanderers and makes it the most deaths in '''Level 611'''. ===Smilers=== '''Smilers''' are less common in '''Level 611''', predominantly in '''Dark Mode''', but they occasionally appear in the southern regions of the '''In-Between State'''.. '''Smilers''' mostly hide in hidden areas such as closets, under the bed, or in small corners and wait for the right moment to attack. Their camouflage makes them more aggressive than other levels, as wanderers are likely to be caught off guard or ambushed by Smilers who come into sight. ===Skin-Stealers=== '''Skin-Stealers''' are less common in '''Level 611''', but they do appear in the '''Dark Mode'''. They are much more aggressive compared to other levels. '''Skin-Stealers''' will attack any wanderers and roam the zone. ===Deathmoths=== '''Deathmoths''' are less common in '''Level 611''' and can usually be found around lights in the '''Dark Mode'''. '''Deathmoths''' are highly drawn to light and can usually be found waiting around. Female '''Deathmoths''' make up the majority of them, and are highly aggressive. Female '''Deathmoths''' are the worst and potentially attack anyone that approaches them too closely. ===Wretches=== '''Wretches''', or referred to as '''Roth Syndrome''', are less common and located in '''Level 611''', specifically in the dark regions of the Dark Zone. Most of them are former wanderers who were once in the '''Dark Mode'''. Wretches are aggressive and would attack wanderers at sight. They are known to ambush and hide wanderers who venture deeper in the '''Dark Mode'''. ===Hounds=== '''Hounds''' are rare in '''Level 611''' and are mostly found in the deeper regions of the '''Dark Mode'''. '''Hounds''' are known for their ability to catch people off guard and ambush them by lying in wait in tiny, secluded areas. Which is more unusual than other entities in other levels, the '''Hounds''' are faster and more vicious, making '''Hounds''' much more aggressive. Their speed and viciousness mean that wanderers who are not cautious in their surroundings can easily fall prey to them. ===Mirrors=== '''Mirrors''' are the rarest to find in '''Level 611''', but occasionally they can be seen in or near the '''Dark Mode'''. These '''Mirrors''' tend to trap wanderers who gaze into them. The mirrors may display childhood memories, pictures of the wanderers' parents or family members, or their favorite locations. Over time, the wanderer's reflection would gradually melt or become distorted, or the wanderer would notice a dark figure behind them. This is dangerous, as the entities in '''Dark mode''' are typically lurking or roaming. Gazing into these '''Mirrors''' causes symptoms, reports of wanderers experiencing such as auditory and visual hallucinations, dizziness, fatigue, and burning sensations. ==Colonies and Outpost== There are no longer any outposts or colonies on this level. All have either moved out of '''Level 611''' due to a series of anomalies in the level, or have been slain in their attempts to flee, mainly in Night Mode, and the eye problems and insomnia from Light Mode. They had no choice but to leave '''Level 611''' due to the intense eye irritation and minor skin burning. If the residents on this level stayed any longer, it could result in intense eye irritation or minor skin burning, and a potential chance for The Wretched Cycle to impose itself upon the residents of the level.This causes a wide range of wanderers to disappear in the level when going to '''Night Mode'''. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Walking through a half-lighted and half-dark hallway with a door at the end. Opening the door will lead a wanderer to this level. *Opening any doors on some levels has a very low chance of leading you here. *Completing an equal number of light and dark spaces in [[Level 602]] has a 50% likelihood of sending one here. *No-clipping through a door in [[Level 298]] has a rare chance of sending one here. *Turning off any lights inside a building in [[Level 161]] has a small chance of transitioning here without the wanderer knowing. ===Exits=== Exits are only accessible in '''Dark Mode''' and will not work if accessed in '''Light Mode''' or the '''In-Between-State''' and will result in being sent to [[The Broken]]. *No-clipping successfully through a mirror will send you to [[Level 26.1]].Only the mirror is not an [[Entity 86|Entity]], or will send one to [[The Void]] *Successfully no-clipping into a stationary screen of a TV will send you to [[Level 12]]. If the TV is off or the screen is different, then one will be sent to [[The Void]]. *No-clipping through very rare Christmas decorations is difficult, but if successful, it will send you to [[Level 633]]. *Exiting through left and right EXIT doors, vice versa, has a slim chance of leading one to [[Level 555]].It is not recommended to try this exit due to most of these doors are [[Predatory Doors]]. *Opening and jumping into a window with a pitch black void will lead you to [[The Void]]. If the window is eerie, void of random objects, opening and jumping will lead you to [[Level 9]].
'''> OPEN THE ARCHIVED VERSION BEFORE THE INCIDENT FILE'''
=Level 611: "Comfortable Housing"= {{Class Habitable}} ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Liminal house living room scene remake by mysteriouspoggers12 dfqe8ei-pre.jpg|caption=An image of Level 611}} '''Level 611''' is a massive suburban house that is estimated to be roughly 892,411.76 km².The level was discovered 8 months before [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]]. The level that stretches on and on in all directions. If a wanderer looks out of any one of the windows, they can see a snowy, icy neighborhood with families of [[Entity 9|Facelings]] playing in the snow and a huge number of cars on the road. The level has a day and night cycle that lasts roughly around 24 hours. The suburban house contains a vast array of rooms which are located in '''Level 611''', including hallways, kitchens, living rooms, bedrooms, bathrooms, and occasionally basements. The temperature is always a comfortable rate of around 20°C (68°F). Doors in the house, however, mostly lead into more hallways, rooms, and endless paths, most of which are dead ends. '''Level 611''' kitchens have food in freezers, fridges, pantries, and cabinets. These foods never go bad or mold. Bedrooms have all sorts of electronics, including computers and tablets, and most have enough beds for sleeping. Not all bedrooms, though, have beds and instead usually have a couch. The living room does have wifi, but the couches are extremely uncomfortable and cause a backache if the wanderer stays long enough. The wanderer can leave by going through either of the two "EXIT DOORS," north or south. Each door leads out in the opposite direction. There are no known exits on '''Level 611''', which is a comparatively new level. [[M.E.G.]] is currently conducting research to discover any potential exits. ==Entities== Adult and child [[Entity 9|Facelings]] have only been confirmed; a wanderer can interact with them, although they can get hostile if a wanderer attacks them. There may be entities hiding deeper in the level, but this has not yet been confirmed, however. ==Colonies and Outposts== A number of colonists and outposts appear to have settled here, although only one has managed to announce its presence. ===The Archivist=== '''The Archivist''' may have up to 30 members. They are tradable and are usually seem friendly. There are rumors that they have a cannibalism behavior on wanderers, but there is no solid evidence to substantiate this. The rumor is currently unconfirmed. '''The Archivist''' will roam the level and will trade with wanderers if necessary. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== The only known access to this level is a half-lit and half-dark passageway containing a door at the opposite end. If one were to open the door, one would be led here. ===Exits=== There are no exit points. If a wanderer or traveler enters this level, they must stay on this level for a lifetime. {{Footnotes}} {{Redirect|Prev=610|Next=612}}{{Collapse|initial=> Open Author & Licensing Information|final=> Close Author & Licensing Information|body= '''Original Author:''' [[User:Cyrixgamer|Cyrixgamer]]
